key_insight: PhyNex is an autonomous LLM-based agent that searches for better computational physics methods while using domain tools to preserve physical consistency.
engineering_relevance: It could reduce the trial-and-error burden in scientific software design by helping engineers and researchers rapidly discover effective algorithms under physical constraints.
method: Uses progressive local search guided by a large language model.
Combines LLM proposals with domain-specific computational tools to enforce validity.
Learns reusable knowledge from both successful and failed search attempts.
evidence_signal: The agent was evaluated on three tasks—dielectric spectrum prediction, Max-Cut heuristic design, and quantum battery charging—and matched or exceeded human-designed baselines with improvements up to 3.8% in spectral similarity, 15.0% in normalized mean cut, and 5.9% in ergotropy at the 80k checkpoint.
watch_item: Performance depends on well-defined, scorable objectives and reliable physics-aware tools, so transfer to less structured problems may be limited.
paper_title: Large Language Model Based Agent for Automated Discovery in Computational Physics
